description: Phase 6 version gate calibration fixtures. Each fixture represents a (user_fix, upstream_commits) pair with known ground truth for whether the user's fix is already present upstream. Used by scripts/spike_version_gate_calibrate.py to tune the LLM prompt and confidence threshold.

fixtures.label: TP
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(parser): handle empty input in tokenize()
fixtures.user_fix_body: When tokenize() was called with an empty string, it raised IndexError on line 42. Guard against empty input and return an empty list.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/parser.py
+++ b/src/parser.py
@@ -40,6 +40,8 @@
 def tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
+    if not text:
+        return []
     first = text[0]
     return _split(text)

upstream_commits.subject: fix(parser): return empty list for empty input to tokenize()
upstream_commits.body: tokenize() crashed with IndexError when called with an empty string. Adds a guard to return [] immediately.

fixtures.rationale: Upstream commit a1b2c3d fixes the exact same bug in the exact same function with the same approach.

fixtures.label: TP
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(auth): session cookie missing secure flag
fixtures.user_fix_body: Session cookies were issued without the Secure flag, making them visible over plain HTTP.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/auth/session.py
+++ b/src/auth/session.py
@@ -20,5 +20,5 @@
 def set_session_cookie(response, token):
-    response.set_cookie('session', token, httponly=True)
+    response.set_cookie('session', token, httponly=True, secure=True)

upstream_commits.subject: feat(auth): harden cookie security
upstream_commits.body: Sets Secure, HttpOnly, and SameSite=Lax on all session cookies to close a CVE-2026-1234 disclosure channel. Refactors set_session_cookie to use a centralized CookieConfig.

fixtures.rationale: Upstream commit b2c3d4e adds the Secure flag via a refactor. Different fix approach but same outcome — cookie now has Secure.

fixtures.label: TP
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(api): rate limiter off-by-one in sliding window
fixtures.user_fix_body: The sliding window rate limiter counted one request too few, allowing burst traffic to exceed the configured limit by 1.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/api/rate_limit.py
+++ b/src/api/rate_limit.py
@@ -50,7 +50,7 @@
 def is_allowed(self, key):
     count = self._count_in_window(key)
-    return count < self.limit
+    return count <= self.limit - 1

upstream_commits.subject: fix(api): correct sliding window rate limiter off-by-one
upstream_commits.body: Sliding window counted count < limit which permits limit+1 requests in the window. Changed to count < limit - 1 so exactly limit requests are allowed. Matches documented behavior.

upstream_commits.subject: test(api): add rate limiter sliding window tests

fixtures.rationale: Upstream commit c3d4e5f is a direct fix for the same off-by-one bug. Different exact expression but identical semantics (both allow `limit` requests, not `limit+1`).

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_body: observation_write was not computing content_hash, so dedup on source+content_hash was never triggered.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/genesis/mcp/memory/observations.py
+++ b/src/genesis/mcp/memory/observations.py
@@ -15,6 +15,9 @@
 async def observation_write(content, source, obs_type, priority):
+    chash = hashlib.sha256((source + content).encode()).hexdigest()
     return await observations.create(
-        content=content, source=source, type=obs_type, priority=priority
+        content=content, source=source, type=obs_type, priority=priority,
+        content_hash=chash
     )

upstream_commits.subject: feat(dashboard): add cost tracking widget
upstream_commits.body: New widget in the dashboard shows per-provider spend over the last 24h.

upstream_commits.subject: refactor(router): simplify provider selection
upstream_commits.body: Removes the obsolete legacy_mode branch from RouterV2.

fixtures.rationale: None of the upstream commits touch observations, dedup, or content_hash. Unrelated work.

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(parser): strip BOM from unicode input
fixtures.user_fix_body: Files with a UTF-8 BOM at the start caused tokenize() to emit a phantom first token. Strip the BOM before tokenizing.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/parser.py
+++ b/src/parser.py
@@ -38,6 +38,8 @@
 def tokenize(text: str) -> list[str]:
+    if text.startswith('\ufeff'):
+        text = text[1:]
     return _split(text)

upstream_commits.subject: fix(parser): handle trailing newline edge case in tokenize
upstream_commits.body: tokenize() was emitting a trailing empty token when the input ended with \n. Strip trailing whitespace before splitting.

upstream_commits.subject: test(parser): add tests for malformed input

fixtures.rationale: Upstream fixed a DIFFERENT parser bug (trailing newline) in the same function. The BOM bug is still present — not addressed anywhere in the log.

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(db): migration runner locks on concurrent start
fixtures.user_fix_body: Two processes calling MigrationRunner.run() simultaneously caused a database is locked error because neither was using BEGIN IMMEDIATE.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/db/migration_runner.py
+++ b/src/db/migration_runner.py
@@ -80,7 +80,7 @@
 async def run(self):
-    conn.execute('BEGIN')
+    conn.execute('BEGIN IMMEDIATE')
fixtures.rationale: User is already on upstream HEAD — no upstream commits since their version. Nothing to match.

fixtures.label: TN_partial
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(router): DLQ orphan items on provider rename
fixtures.user_fix_body: When a provider is renamed, items in the DLQ referencing the old name are never cleaned up. This PR scans the DLQ on provider config reload AND on router startup.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/router/dlq.py
+++ b/src/router/dlq.py
@@ -100,4 +100,12 @@
 async def on_config_reload(self):
+    await self._prune_orphan_items()
+
+async def on_startup(self):
+    await self._prune_orphan_items()
+
+async def _prune_orphan_items(self):
+    known = set(self._provider_registry.names())
+    await self._db.execute('DELETE FROM dlq WHERE provider NOT IN (' + ','.join('?' * len(known)) + ')', list(known))

upstream_commits.subject: fix(router): DLQ orphan items after provider config reload
upstream_commits.body: Adds orphan cleanup on config reload. Does NOT cover the startup case — a restart still sees orphan items from configs that changed while the router was down.

upstream_commits.subject: test(router): DLQ orphan cleanup integration test

fixtures.rationale: Upstream fixed HALF the bug (config reload path only). The startup path is still broken. User's fix is strictly MORE complete — should be contributed. This is the tricky case: the LLM must recognize the scope difference.

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(cc_relay): session checkpoint race on concurrent writes
fixtures.user_fix_body: Two CC sessions writing checkpoints at the same time could corrupt the checkpoint file. Use a lock file.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/cc_relay/checkpoint.py
+++ b/src/cc_relay/checkpoint.py
@@ -45,3 +45,7 @@
 def save(self, data):
+    with filelock.FileLock(self.path + '.lock'):
         self.path.write_text(json.dumps(data))

upstream_commits.subject: fix(cc_relay): serialize checkpoint writes with filelock
upstream_commits.body: Adds filelock around checkpoint.save to prevent concurrent-write corruption.

upstream_commits.subject: revert: "fix(cc_relay): serialize checkpoint writes with filelock"
upstream_commits.body: Reverts a7b8c9d. The filelock import introduced a circular dependency with cc_relay.init and broke startup. Will be re-applied after the dependency is untangled.

fixtures.rationale: Upstream applied AND reverted the fix. At upstream HEAD the bug is still present. The LLM should notice the revert and not cancel.

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_subject: fix(reflection): light cycle skips SOUL.md injection
fixtures.user_fix_body: LightReflection._build_prompt() was reading USER.md but not SOUL.md. Added SOUL.md to the identity file load loop.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/reflection/light.py
+++ b/src/reflection/light.py
@@ -30,5 +30,5 @@
 def _build_prompt(self):
-    for name in ['USER.md', 'CONVERSATION.md']:
+    for name in ['SOUL.md', 'USER.md', 'CONVERSATION.md']:
         content = self._load_identity(name)

upstream_commits.subject: fix(reflection): light cycle uses wrong prompt template on retry
upstream_commits.body: After a retry, LightReflection._build_prompt() was using the generic template instead of the light-specific template. Fixed to always use the light template.

fixtures.rationale: Upstream fixed a DIFFERENT bug in the same _build_prompt function (template selection on retry). The SOUL.md omission is still present. LLM must not confuse 'touched same function' with 'fixed same bug'.

fixtures.label: TN
fixtures.user_fix_body: Ollama embedding endpoint returned 400 when input exceeded the context window. Chunk the input and embed each chunk separately, then average the vectors.
fixtures.user_fix_diff:
--- a/src/providers/ollama.py
+++ b/src/providers/ollama.py
@@ -60,3 +60,10 @@
 async def embed(self, text: str) -> list[float]:
-    return await self._client.embed(text)
+    if len(text) > self.MAX_INPUT_CHARS:
+        chunks = self._chunk(text, self.MAX_INPUT_CHARS)
+        vecs = await asyncio.gather(*[self._client.embed(c) for c in chunks])
+        return self._average(vecs)
+    return await self._client.embed(text)

upstream_commits.subject: fix(ollama): skip embedding when input is too long
upstream_commits.body: Workaround for the 400 error on long inputs: return None instead of crashing. Callers now handle the None case by falling back to FTS5-only search. Proper chunking is a larger change for later.

fixtures.rationale: Upstream did a WORKAROUND (return None, fall back to FTS5), not a real fix. User's fix is the proper chunking solution — should be contributed. This is another scope/completeness difference.
fixtures.notes: If the LLM is overly eager to match similar commit subjects, it might cancel this one wrongly.
